_instructions: Source of truth for all agent decisions. Do not modify field names — only values.
_experience_note: MS student graduating 2026 with 2 years of real professional experience across data, ML, and full-stack roles. Not a traditional new grad — has production deployments and verifiable impact metrics.

education._university_alias_note: Use university_aliases for resumes, cover letters, emails, and LLM prompts. form_only_legacy_names are for outdated application portals only — never use them in prose.

bullets: Built automated Python ETL pipelines to ingest, clean, and standardize multi-source operational data
Developed Power BI dashboards delivering real-time KPIs and business insights for stakeholders
Reduced operational errors by 70%+ by deploying a production data system integrating REST APIs
Collaborated cross-functionally with operations, sales, and marketing teams to drive data-driven decisions
Designed and implemented REST API integrations and automated backend data workflows
Built scalable data pipelines using webhooks and schema validation across systems
Delivered production-ready applications with real-time data integration and analytics instrumentation
Maintained version-controlled codebases with Git/GitHub and full documentation
Built MRI brain tumor classification model using MobileNetV2 achieving 86.6% accuracy
Developed real-time computer vision pipeline using OpenCV with >85% accuracy
Applied data preprocessing, augmentation, and transfer learning techniques in ML pipelines
Collaborated via GitHub on code reviews and distributed team workflows

application_questions.why_this_company_template: I'm drawn to {company} because of its work in {specific_area}. My background building production ML systems — including a forecasting model hitting ~92% accuracy and ETL pipelines that cut operational errors by 70% — aligns directly with the {role}. I want to build systems that have measurable impact, and {company} is where I want to do that.
application_questions.biggest_strength: I close the gap between research and production. I've shipped ML models, ETL pipelines, and full-stack dashboards in real working environments — not just academic settings. I move fast, document well, and care deeply about whether things actually work in production.
application_questions.biggest_weakness: I tend to want to understand systems deeply before modifying them, which can slow initial ramp-up. I've gotten much better at balancing thoroughness with speed and knowing when it's fine to build on top of something without understanding every layer.
application_questions.where_do_you_see_yourself: In 5 years I want to be a senior engineer or lead data scientist who has shipped multiple high-impact ML systems end-to-end — not just models, but the pipelines, APIs, and dashboards that make them useful in production.
application_questions.why_data_science: I'm drawn to problems where the answer isn't obvious until you look at the data. Building the NYC collision risk model across 1M+ records and surfacing patterns that weren't visible before — that's what gets me excited. I want to do that at scale.
application_questions.tell_me_about_yourself: I'm a Data Science MS candidate at Texas A&M finishing in 2026. Over the last two years I've built in production — ETL pipelines that cut operational errors by 70%, full-stack data integrations, and ML models with real accuracy benchmarks. My strength is connecting data science to engineering: I don't just build models, I build the systems that make those models useful.
application_questions.work_authorization: I am authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship.
application_questions.disability_status: I do not have a disability.
application_questions.cover_letter_hook: As a Data Science MS candidate at Texas A&M with production experience building ML pipelines, ETL systems, and full-stack analytics platforms, I'm excited to bring both technical depth and a track record of measurable impact to {company}.
